142684.explanation: The council has that possibility and must be able to deploy it if necessary.

142685.explanation: We must be able to guarantee uniform quality of maintenance and management. Furthermore, any possible financial consequences must not be borne by the residents.

142687.explanation: Not always. The municipality must always investigate whether it is possible.

142688.explanation: In this case, yes, because that financial support is currently possible and provided by the national government.

142689.explanation: In the case of Rucphen, yes. We are too small in nature and size to implement this. In our neighboring municipalities, this might be better suited.

142692.explanation: This depends on the situation. For example, whether a replanting obligation is necessary or not.

142693.explanation: The principle must be that no nuisance should be caused by the use.

142695.explanation: We must ensure that our residents can continue to enjoy fireworks and tradition despite possible national bans.

142696.explanation: Collection of bulky household waste is already possible, but this involves costs for all parties. In this case, the polluter pays.

142699.explanation: It depends on the circumstances of each case. However, we believe the principle should be: yes, provided, instead of no, unless.

142700.explanation: This is already happening at the moment, for example via the adult fund.

142701.explanation: Where possible, this should definitely take place. The local broadcaster plays an important role in informing our residents.

142703.explanation: This is already happening at the moment.

142704.explanation: This is already happening at the moment.

142707.explanation: Safety for all our residents is paramount.

142708.explanation: Safety is paramount but must never come at the expense of residents' privacy!

142710.explanation: This is already happening at the moment.

142713.explanation: Given the situation in the housing market, creative solutions are needed.

142716.explanation: Where possible, we must help residents who really need it.

142717.explanation: Residents who genuinely need help must receive it. We should look at the practical reality and not just percentages.

142719.explanation: Connections must be made based on the demand for charging points.

142720.explanation: Where this improves traffic safety and flow, it should be implemented. This also applies to 50 km/h zones.

142721.explanation: It concerns the necessity of receiving care.

142722.explanation: The price/quality ratio must be good. So do not only focus on the price.

142723.explanation: The principle is that costs should remain as low as possible. However, this does not mean that increases are impossible.

142726.explanation: This does not apply to all waste. The polluter pays, which means certain waste streams must simply be charged.

142728.explanation: The principle is that every village center should have a community center where residents can gather. However, an investigation is needed to determine whether the current location is future-proof and whether combinations with other social buildings are possible.

142729.explanation: We are already above the national average. In our municipality, the demand for mid-rent housing is greater than for social rent.

142684.explanation: Citizens already have influence through elections. Elected representatives are busy every day with rules and laws, weighing interests, and making compromises. A referendum would undermine this system.

142685.explanation: Residents know best what is needed locally. If users are responsible themselves, the supply better matches the demand.

142687.explanation: Tailored solutions must be provided for what is possible. For one company this can be a shop, for another a campsite.

142688.explanation: The development of energy hubs is the future. It is important that this is feasible and affordable, so initial financial support is necessary.

142689.explanation: Large-scale distribution centers should not be placed in small rural municipalities. Local entrepreneurs should, however, be facilitated.

142692.explanation: The replanting obligation has already been introduced in the municipality of Rucphen.

142693.explanation: People should be free to choose how they heat their homes and create atmosphere.

142695.explanation: The municipality must facilitate companies to organize fireworks shows. An official fireworks show reduces the risks of private fireworks and thus fire and injury hazards.

142696.explanation: The polluter pays. Bulky waste can already be delivered at the Saver resource center. This is mostly free of charge.

142698.explanation: Accessibility is part of good entrepreneurship; entrepreneurs must invest in their own buildings.

142699.explanation: New homes may only be built if they replace existing buildings, so that the rural character and agricultural land are preserved.

142700.explanation: The municipality should not automatically pay sports club memberships for welfare recipients but can offer support in individual cases. The Leergeld Foundation already supports families with children.

142701.explanation: The local broadcaster no longer exists.

142703.explanation: A personal contribution from parents will remain necessary.

142704.explanation: Sports clubs are already financially supported.

142707.explanation: There are currently six enforcement officers (BOAs) for five church villages.

142708.explanation: If necessary, this should be expanded.

142710.explanation: This loan already exists, and we are in favor of expanding these facilities.

142713.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for tourism; permanent residence can affect the character of a recreational area.

142716.explanation: People with low incomes cannot always pay their taxes; waivers prevent financial problems.

142717.explanation: The standard should be the same nationwide at the rural level.

142719.explanation: Electric mobility is increasing, so being prepared is practical.

142720.explanation: Tailored measures are necessary: reduced speed only on streets with many children, schools, residential areas, and cyclists, where safety is clearly an issue.

142721.explanation: Who provides the care is not relevant; it is more important that care can be provided.

142722.explanation: The balance between price and quality is essential.

142723.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is already the lowest in the region. Increases only with inflation correction.

142726.explanation: Everyone pays for the costs of their own waste; offering it for free can lead to waste or abuse.

142728.explanation: This is combined with the new construction of the school in Zegge. An integral plan of the entire area including an event location.

142729.explanation: We are against fixed social rental quotas in new construction and want to emphasize market forces and practical feasibility.

142684.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a referendum. Since the municipal council is elected by its residents, the council makes decisions weighing all pros and cons. It is important to involve residents in major issues.

142685.explanation: The CDA supports initiatives in which residents participate.

142687.explanation: The CDA strongly supports more space for other businesses around farms.

142688.explanation: The CDA supports energy cooperatives and neighborhood residents who take initiatives to make the environment more sustainable. The CDA believes it is the municipality's task to support this in many ways.

142689.explanation: The CDA believes our municipality is not suitable for establishing large distribution centers (large box-shaped buildings).

142692.explanation: We want a green and livable municipality. Therefore, the CDA has previously proposed planting a new tree in the village where every felled tree is cut down.

142693.explanation: The CDA considers a burn ban unacceptable.

142695.explanation: The CDA wants to keep the option for fireworks on New Year's Eve, even with a private fireworks ban. This is possible because the municipality allows fireworks to be set off collectively by associations.

142696.explanation: Nothing is free. Waste processing costs money. We pay waste disposal charges for that. The CDA wants to keep these charges as low as possible.

142698.explanation: The municipality has a task to make public spaces accessible. The CDA allocates funds for this. Keeping business buildings accessible is the responsibility of the businesses themselves.

142699.explanation: The CDA wants to address the housing shortage, including on agricultural land, provided this does not hinder other residents.

142700.explanation: This is already policy and the CDA is in favor of it.

142701.explanation: The local broadcaster receives a contribution from the municipality. Nationally, there is a proposal to provide extra funding to local broadcasters from the central government. The CDA supports the government covering these costs.

142703.explanation: The municipality offers children the opportunity to get acquainted with music, sports, and culture. The CDA considers it the parents' responsibility to further finance this. Of course, low-income families can apply for assistance.

142704.explanation: The municipality already financially contributes to sports facilities and subsidizes clubs. It is primarily up to sports clubs themselves to find solutions for possible problems. The CDA does see a supporting role for the municipality in this.

142707.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. More enforcement officers (BOA's) on the streets and in rural areas contribute to this.

142708.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. This can be done through additional camera surveillance in unsafe places.

142710.explanation: This scheme is already in effect partly thanks to the CDA.

142713.explanation: There are quite a number of holiday homes in our municipality. When these are used for permanent residence, it negatively affects recreational facilities. The CDA supports recreational opportunities.

142716.explanation: The municipality must show a humane face: waiving municipal taxes is one of the poverty reduction measures municipalities can provide.

142717.explanation: The current (higher) income limits are set regionally and according to the CDA are sufficiently socially justified and do not require adjustments.

142719.explanation: The CDA finds it important that there are sufficient charging points for electric cars. These should be placed based on demand, where people with electric cars live and work, and near public buildings.

142720.explanation: The CDA wants you to be able to drive safely at 50 km/h on the main roads within urban areas. The standard in residential streets is 30 km/h.

142721.explanation: The CDA regards care providers and professionals as neutral. It concerns a service, and personal beliefs are irrelevant.

142722.explanation: The CDA considers cost control important, but it must never come at the expense of the quality of youth care delivery.

142723.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of an increase in the property tax, but where costs rise, it may be necessary for the property tax to rise in line with inflation.

142726.explanation: Nothing is free. Waste processing costs money, for which we pay a waste levy. The CDA wants to keep these levies as low as possible.

142728.explanation: According to the CDA, there is no reason to renew the SKW de Nieuwenbergh. Any potential renewal of village halls should be investigated in an integrated manner.

142729.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of fixed municipal percentages. The CDA finds it important to build types of housing that primarily consider seniors and starters.

142693.explanation: Depending on the location and weather conditions, residents can make a considered choice on whether it is responsible to light a fireplace or wood stove.

142695.explanation: Fireworks on New Year's Eve account for 5% of the total nitrogen emissions, which must already be reduced.

142696.explanation: The polluter pays. If bulky waste is picked up for free, illegal dumping by companies will also occur.

142698.explanation: This is the responsibility of the entrepreneur. We would prefer this to be mandatory for entrepreneurs.

142699.explanation: Building is indeed necessary, but there must be no uncontrolled growth in the rural area.

142703.explanation: Music is an important part of cultural education.

142704.explanation: Sports are important for health and should be encouraged.

142713.explanation: This depends on the type of holiday home and its location. Often access roads are unsuitable for frequent daily use, making a safe traffic situation unguaranteed.

142719.explanation: Additional charging points will need to be installed where necessary.

142720.explanation: This is already the case in our municipality.

142722.explanation: The focus should primarily be on high-quality care at an affordable price.

142726.explanation: We believe in the principle 'the polluter pays'; recyclable products should still be able to be returned free of charge.

142728.explanation: The municipality must support local community work where needed.

142729.explanation: The municipality of Rucphen already has 30% social housing. There must be sufficient mixed construction in the categories where there is demand.

statements.title: Rucphen should renovate the village community centre De Nieuwenbergh in Zegge.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The residents of Rucphen should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Rucphen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.
There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Rucphen who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet ondernemers financieel helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.
In Rucphen the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Rucphen moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
There should be more camera surveillance in Rucphen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het kwijtschelden van de

titleParts.glossary: Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: At least 30% of newly built homes in Rucphen must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, currently 28% of homes are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Rucphen moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.

shootoutStatements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be free of charge.
